author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
verses:
āmāra emana bhāgya kata dine ha’be
āmāre āpana bali’ jānibe vaiṣṇave

śrī⋅gurucaraṇāmṛta⋅mādhvika⋅sevane
matta ha’ye kṛṣṇa-guṇa gā’ba vṛndāvane

karmī, jñānī, kṛṣṇadveṣī bahirmukha⋅jana
ghṛṇā kari’ akiñcane karibe varjana

karmajaḍa⋅smārtagaṇa karibe siddhānta
ācāra⋅rahita ei nitānta aśānta

vātula baliyā more paṇḍitābhimānī
tyajibe āmāra saṅga māyāvādī jñānī

kusaṅga⋅rahita dekhi’ vaiṣṇava⋅sujana
kṛpā kari’ āmāre dibena āliṅgana

sparśiyā vaiṣṇava⋅deha e durjana chāra
ānande labhibe kabe sāttvika vikāra
en_translation:
When will such good fortune be mine? When will the Vaiṣṇavas consider me their own?

By serving the honey-sweet nectar of śrī-guru's feet, Becoming absorbed, I will sing the glories of Kṛṣṇa in Vṛndāvana.

The those engaged in fruitive activities, those engaged in speculative knowledge, those envious of Kṛṣṇa, and those averse to Kṛṣṇa consciousness Will scornfully reject this one who considers himself to possess nothing.

The smārta-brāhmaṇas rigid in fruitive rituals will declare, "This person is devoid of proper conduct and utterly restless."

Considering me a madman, those proud of their scholarship, The impersonalists philosophers will abandon my company.

Seeing me devoid of bad company, the good devotees Will mercifully embrace me.

When will this wicked and insignificant wretch, by touching a body of a Vaiṣṇava, Joyfully attain spiritual transformations?
